title: bursera heterophylla
intro: Bursera heterophylla, also known as the "Ocopa" or " Copal Tree", is a tropical plant species native to Central and South America, particularly in the regions of Mexico, Guatemala, and Costa Rica. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress. The health benefits of Bursera heterophylla include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis, as well as its ability to support immune system function and protect against infections. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
